skill.name: typescript-review
skill.description: Review TypeScript and JavaScript code changes for compliance with Metabase coding standards, style violations, and code quality issues. Use when reviewing pull requests or diffs containing TypeScript/JavaScript code.
skill.summary: Review TypeScript and JavaScript diffs for Metabase standards, type safety, React practices, and test readiness.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged six external command references and one high weak-crypto pattern. The command references are hardcoded Yarn review commands, and no cryptographic operation is present on the flagged line. The skill is safe to publish with a low external-command warning.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Local Review Commands
low_findings.description: The skill documents Yarn commands for linting, formatting, type checking, and tests. These are legitimate review actions, but they require Bash access and may execute project scripts in the local repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible as fixed Yarn invocations with no interpolation or user-controlled shell construction. The residual risk is local script execution through Bash, not command injection.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static high finding on the skill description line does not correspond to any cryptographic algorithm or hashing call. No evidence found for weak cryptography in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive text about TypeScript and JavaScript review. There is no crypto-related code or instruction at that location.

content.user_title: Review TypeScript Changes Against Metabase Standards
content.value_statement: TypeScript pull requests can miss project conventions, type safety issues, and React review details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused Metabase-style review checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews TypeScript and JavaScript diffs against Metabase coding standards.
Prompts lint, formatting, type-check, and targeted test workflows.
Checks for code quality, JSDoc clarity, and type safety issues.
Highlights React best practices when applicable to changed files.
Supports focused pull request review for changed frontend code.
limitations: It is tailored to Metabase workflows and command names.
It depends on repository scripts being available and correctly configured.
It does not replace maintainer judgment for architecture or product behavior.
It only covers TypeScript, JavaScript, React, and related ClojureScript test guidance.

use_cases.title: Review a frontend pull request
use_cases.description: Check changed TypeScript or JavaScript files for standards, type safety, tests, and React-specific review concerns.

use_cases.title: Prepare code before requesting review
use_cases.description: Run through lint, formatting, type checking, and targeted tests before handing a branch to maintainers.

use_cases.title: Standardize reviewer feedback
use_cases.description: Use a consistent checklist for JSDoc quality, code clarity, TypeScript usage, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a small TypeScript or JavaScript diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my changed TypeScript and JavaScript files using the typescript-review skill. Focus on obvious standards, type safety, and test gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: You want a structured review before opening a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-review skill to create a pull request review checklist for my current diff. Include lint, formatting, type checking, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: React-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Your diff changes React components or hooks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the typescript-review skill to my React changes. Focus on component design, hook usage, type safety, JSDoc, and targeted tests.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Maintainer Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You need concise maintainer-grade findings for a larger diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request with the typescript-review skill. Prioritize behavioral risks, standards violations, missing tests, and actionable maintainer feedback.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes a React component and a TypeScript helper.
output: One finding explains a type narrowing issue that can hide an undefined value.
One recommendation asks for a targeted unit test covering the changed helper behavior.
The review notes that formatting and lint checks should pass before merge.

output_examples.input: A contributor asks for a pre-review checklist.
output: Run the repository lint command for JavaScript and TypeScript files.
Run the formatter and inspect any changed files.
Run type checking and the most relevant unit test command.

best_practices: Run the suggested checks from a clean working tree when possible.
Review the diff before running broad test commands.
Treat findings as review input, then verify behavior in the application.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad commands without understanding local project scripts.
Do not accept style-only changes that hide behavioral regressions.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for product or accessibility review.

faq.question: What code does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews TypeScript and JavaScript changes, with extra guidance for React code when relevant.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It lists Yarn commands that an assistant may run when Bash access is available and appropriate.

faq.question: Is this only for Metabase?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance and command names are tailored to the Metabase repository.

faq.question: Can it find every bug?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on standards, type safety, code quality, documentation, and test signals.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The skill documents fixed local Yarn commands. There is no evidence of malicious behavior or data exfiltration.
